summary: Iteration 4 (Westwood Ranch western theme + seed + page transitions) fully verified. Backend pytest 34/34 green — added TestThemeField (3 tests: default theme, create with western, update theme persists) and TestSeedIdempotent (3 tests: exact Westwood Ranch shape with passcode 1976/color #C2410C/icon Crown/3 sections, 9 seeded media with 3 per section + mix of direct & embed, double-invocation of _seed_westwood_ranch produces zero duplicates). No regression on iter-1/2/3 endpoints. Frontend Playwright verified all 12 review-request items: (1) Home shows Bob + Westwood Ranch cards; (2) Westwood card opens passcode dialog with Crown icon & burnt-orange '#C2410C' accent; (3) Passcode 1976 navigates to /profile/{id}; (4) [data-testid=western-hero-banner] visible with [data-testid=western-hero-title]='Westwood Ranch' and [data-testid=western-hero-subtitle] present, root carries data-theme=western; (5) Section h2 computed font-family is 'Playfair Display', serif, all 9 cards render across 3 sections, '3 ITEMS' counter visible; (6) Non-western Bob profile has NO hero, h2 uses Outfit sans-serif; (7) Clicking a seeded YouTube card opens VideoPlayer overlay with [data-testid=player-iframe]; (8) Page transitions work (/, /profile/{id}, /admin all resolve cleanly with AnimatePresence); (9) Visual polish: mobile 375px and desktop 1280px both render Westwood hero+grid, Bob profile, Admin Login keypad and Home grid with no broken layouts; (10) Sonner toaster + dialogs use consistent dark palette. No new console errors of substance.
critical_code_review_comments: Carry-over from iter 3: server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers (pytest emits DeprecationWarning for both).

Hero background image source is a single hard-coded Unsplash URL — if that asset is ever removed/blocked, hero still renders due to the overlay gradient, but consider self-hosting in /app/frontend/public for resilience.

Profile.theme is a free-form string (max 30 chars) on the Pydantic model — no whitelist enum. A bogus value (e.g. theme='zzz') simply falls back to default-shell rendering on FE. Acceptable for now but a Literal['default','western'] enum on the API would be safer.
ProfilePublic in /api/profiles/{id}/verify will silently coerce missing theme to 'default' via the model default — good defensive behavior; keep this when adding more themes.

Section count badge code reads 'item/items' (lowercase) and relies on CSS `uppercase` to render '3 ITEMS'. Tests assert lowercase substring; just be aware screen readers will hear 'three items' (fine).
test_credentials: Master admin passcode: 1115. Westwood Ranch profile passcode: 1976. Both confirmed against /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Westwood Ranch profile is server-seeded on startup (theme=western, 9 media items across Best Moments/Classic Scenes/Hidden Gems). Tests are idempotent and clean up TEST_-prefixed profiles in the module fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 test ids in use: western-hero-banner, western-hero-title, western-hero-subtitle. Root container of ProfileShell carries data-theme='western' when profile.theme==='western', else 'default'. Section h2 font-family computed string is "Playfair Display", serif on western and Outfit, sans-serif elsewhere — assert via getComputedStyle, not className. Counter label is 'X items' lowercase in DOM but rendered uppercase via CSS. To reseed in tests, import server._seed_westwood_ranch and run via asyncio.run (already done in TestSeedIdempotent.test_reseed_does_not_create_duplicates). Westwood Ranch and Bob profiles are persisted across runs; the cleanup teardown only deletes TEST_-prefixed profiles, by design.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 4.
